summary: Matched Mewtwo Disable item logic by replacing placeholders in itmewtwodisable.c with typed item spawn, destroy, owner cleanup, velocity/lifetime setup, animation, collision, reflection, and event-reference handling code. The PR also promoted itmewtwodisable.c from NonMatching to Matching, renamed two previously address-style symbols to behavior-based Logic67 names, and added the Mewtwo Disable item attribute/item-var structs needed to type the implementation.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itmewtwodisable.c is built as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).

Implemented previously placeholder or incomplete Mewtwo Disable item functions in src/melee/it/items/itmewtwodisable.c, including destroy, destroyed callback, spawn, motion setup, animation, collision, reflected, and event-reference cleanup behavior.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F7750 with one state using itMewtwodisable_UnkMotion0_Anim, itMewtwodisable_UnkMotion0_Phys, and itMewtwodisable_UnkMotion0_Coll.
Renamed it_802C49E0 to it_2725_Logic67_Destroy and it_802C4A40 to it_2725_Logic67_SpawnMewtwoDisable in symbols.txt, headers, and Mewtwo fighter special-low call sites.
Updated ftMt_SpecialLw.c so Mewtwo removes or spawns the Disable item through the new typed behavior names rather than raw address names.
Added itMDisableAttributes with lifetime and x_vel fields and itMDisable_ItemVars with a Fighter_GObj* owner field to itCommonItems.h.
Added mdisable to Item.xDD4_itemVar union in src/melee/it/types.h.
Corrected itmewtwodisable.h prototypes to use Item_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, Vec3*, float, and void/bool/int return types where evidence in the diff showed concrete signatures.
smart_moves: Named the public item functions around item logic behavior and kind, e.g. it_2725_Logic67_Destroy and it_2725_Logic67_SpawnMewtwoDisable, which made fighter call sites self-documenting.
Introduced a specific xDD4_itemVar.mdisable owner field instead of relying on untyped storage, enabling clean owner tracking and callback cleanup.
Linked item lifetime and movement setup to article special attributes through itMDisableAttributes, avoiding magic constants in the matched item logic.

Preserved owner-reference safety by clearing the fighter's stored Disable GObj in it_2725_Logic67_Destroyed and clearing item owner state in it_2725_Logic67_EvtUnk when the referenced fighter matches.

Used existing item engine helpers rather than open-coding systems: Item_80268B18 for spawn, Item_8026A8EC for destroy, it_80275158 for lifetime, Item_80268E5C for animation state, it_802755C0 for scaling, and it_8026B894 for interaction-reference removal.
Accounted for Mewtwo scale in both projectile velocity and item visual scaling via ftLib_80086A0C.
decomp_lessons: For character-owned projectiles, matching often requires typing both the item special attributes and the per-item xDD4 union state before the item logic can become clean C.
When an item is stored on a fighter variable such as fv.mt.x222C_disableGObj, both sides of the relationship need cleanup paths: fighter-driven removal and item-destroyed callback cleanup.
Behavior-based symbol names should be propagated through symbols.txt, item headers, and fighter call sites in the same PR to keep the codebase searchable and avoid stale address names.

SpawnItem initialization order and field values are important matching evidence: this implementation sets kind, prev_pos, position via it_8026BB68, facing_dir, damage, zero velocity components, parent gobj fields, x44_flag.b0, and x40 before calling Item_80268B18.
Small no-op or always-true callbacks should still be given typed parameter names like item_gobj; this helps document the item callback table even when behavior is trivial.
Collision callbacks can be matched by composing existing collision-report helpers; here wall and ceiling reports are ORed after general collision processing.
naming_conventions: Logic67 corresponds to Mewtwo Disable item behavior in the item logic naming scheme, as shown by names such as it_2725_Logic67_Destroy, it_2725_Logic67_Destroyed, it_2725_Logic67_DmgDealt, and it_2725_Logic67_EvtUnk.
Use item-specific struct names with an it-prefixed item abbreviation: itMDisableAttributes and itMDisable_ItemVars.
Prefer Item_GObj* item_gobj and Fighter_GObj* fighter_gobj/ref_owner parameter names over generic gobj or arg0 when signatures are understood.
Spawn helper names may include the originating fighter and move/item name when they replace an address symbol, e.g. it_2725_Logic67_SpawnMewtwoDisable.
State callback names retained the existing item object naming pattern: itMewtwodisable_UnkMotion0_Anim, itMewtwodisable_UnkMotion0_Phys, and itMewtwodisable_UnkMotion0_Coll.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted raw address symbols to typed declarations and updated all call sites, which is a common prerequisite for a matching C implementation.
Used a temporary pointer Vec3* vel = &spawnitem.vel before zeroing velocity components, suggesting local-shape/order sensitivity for matching.
Used a temporary Fighter_GObj** owner pointer before assigning *owner = item->owner in the spawn function, likely to preserve the original codegen shape.
Kept chained zero assignment item->x40_vel.y = item->x40_vel.z = 0.0f and explicit lifetime decrement by 1.0f in the animation callback.
Implemented collision return as an accumulated u32 coll_flags initialized to 0, ORing it_80276308 and it_802763E0 after it_8026D9A0.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body credits the work to @Savestate2A03 on Discord and links a decomp.me scratch: https://decomp.me/scratch/UeMA1.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename it_802C4B38 or it_802C4BB8 to behavior-based Mewtwo Disable names.
Search for other character-owned projectile items that use xDD4_itemVar owner cleanup patterns similar to mdisable.owner.
Compare Mewtwo Shadow Ball matching work for shared Mewtwo projectile spawn, owner, scale, and article attribute conventions.
Check whether itMewtwodisable_UnkMotion0_Coll return type was later normalized between int and bool in headers or call tables.
